text:
There once was a tiny village where everyone sang. From morning until night, the villagers would pour their hearts into a song that would fill the air and resonate in their chests. The songs varied—poems about the harvest, legends of legendary heroes, or simple lullabies for sleepy villagers.

One day, an old bard named Galen approached the village. He was unlike any the villagers had ever seen. His hair shone with an ethereal glow. He wore a cloak made entirely of shadows. Despite his mysterious appearance, the villagers quickly learned that Galen was a true bard, and his songs were enchanting.

Galen decided he wanted to join in their tradition. He gathered the villagers in the meadow, his voice weaving spells that made flowers bloom faster. The villagers were awestruck, and Galen sang something profound about unity and harmony.

As the songs continued, a mysterious wind seemed to carry their melodies away. The villagers watched in awe, unsure where they went.

Days
